open_source.name: Liney
open_source.pitch: A native macOS terminal workspace for multi-repo workflows.

open_source.name: MicroClaw
open_source.pitch: An agentic AI assistant that lives in your chats.

open_source.name: atosl-rs
open_source.pitch: A Rust replacement for Apple's atos — runs on Linux and macOS.

open_source.name: MachOExplorer
open_source.pitch: Explore Mach-O binary files on macOS and Windows.

open_source.name: AppleTrace
open_source.pitch: Trace Objective-C method calls and visualize the call chart.

apps.name: SpeakTwo
apps.pitch: Real-time speech translation between two people, on one iPhone.

apps.name: Lectern
apps.pitch: Search, watch, and learn from WWDC sessions with synced transcripts.

apps.name: RepoRead
apps.pitch: Read GitHub repositories as a book — no clone, no setup.

apps.name: ScriptWidget
apps.pitch: Build iOS, iPadOS and macOS widgets with JavaScript and JSX.

apps.name: StartMyApp
apps.pitch: A keyboard-first app launcher for macOS with Apple Intelligence.

apps.name: Translayr
apps.pitch: System-wide translation across every macOS app, powered by local AI.

apps.name: PDFRead
apps.pitch: Read PDFs in any language, sentence by sentence, side by side.

apps.name: Wizvoid
apps.pitch: Run large language models entirely offline on your iPhone.

apps.name: CountMyDays
apps.pitch: Countdowns and day-counters in beautiful, offline-first cards.

apps.name: MyJSONDiff
apps.pitch: A simple, native JSON diff tool for macOS.

apps.name: Remoboard
apps.pitch: Type on your iPhone using the keyboard of your computer.

apps.name: BSSIDScan
apps.pitch: Discover and analyze WiFi networks around you.

apps.name: NotBadBookmark
apps.pitch: A modern, fast replacement for Chrome's bookmark manager.

bssidscan.name: BSSIDScan
bssidscan.tagline: Discover and analyze the WiFi networks around you.
bssidscan.description: BSSIDScan lists every network in range, with their BSSIDs, security types, channels and signal strengths. Useful for diagnosing flaky home WiFi, picking a quiet channel, or just being curious about what's broadcasting nearby.

features.title: BSSID discovery
features.body: See the BSSID of every network in range, not just SSIDs.

features.title: Security details
features.body: WEP, WPA2, WPA3 — see what each network is using.

features.title: Live signal strength
features.body: Refreshes constantly so you can find the strongest spot in the room.

features.title: Channel info
features.body: Spot crowded channels at a glance.

features.title: Sort & filter
features.body: By name, channel, security, or signal strength.

features.title: No login
features.body: Open it, scan. That's it.

countmydays.name: CountMyDays
countmydays.tagline: Days counted up, days counted down — in plain, beautiful cards.
countmydays.description: CountMyDays is for the dates worth remembering and the dates worth waiting for. Add a card, pick a color, and watch the number tick. Everything lives on your device. No accounts, no cloud, no analytics.

features.title: Count up or down
features.body: Track how long since something happened, or how long until something will.

features.title: Gradient cards
features.body: Pick a color and a style. Pin favorites to the top.

features.title: Offline-first
features.body: All data stays on your device. Nothing syncs unless you ask.

features.title: Import & export
features.body: Move your cards between devices via a JSON file you own.

features.title: Widgets
features.body: Surface the dates you care about on the home screen.

features.title: Free
features.body: No subscriptions. No upsells.

jsondiff.name: MyJSONDiff
jsondiff.tagline: A simple, native JSON diff tool for macOS.
jsondiff.description: Paste two JSON documents into the panes; the differences are highlighted side by side in real time. Auto-formats, validates, and remembers your last few comparisons. Useful when you need an answer in five seconds and don't want to open a tab.

links.label: Download on the Mac App Store

features.title: Side-by-side view
features.body: Two synchronized panes with line-level highlighting.

features.title: Live diff
features.body: Edits on either side re-diff instantly as you type.

features.title: Auto-format & validate
features.body: Pasted JSON is pretty-printed and validation errors are obvious.

features.title: Keyboard friendly
features.body: Open, paste, focus, swap — all with shortcuts.

features.title: Native performance
features.body: Built in SwiftUI. Opens instantly, idles cheaply.

features.title: No cloud
features.body: Your payloads never leave the app. No telemetry.

lectern.name: Lectern - Watch & Learn
lectern.tagline: Search, watch, and learn from WWDC sessions.
lectern.description: Lectern helps developers browse Apple developer videos by year and technology, search across session titles and transcripts, and jump directly to the moment a topic is mentioned. It keeps the learning workflow in one native app with local indexing, favorites, synced transcripts, and optional AI assistance.

features.title: Transcript-first search
features.body: Search titles and transcripts with local full-text indexing, then jump straight to the matching timestamp.

features.title: Native session browser
features.body: Browse WWDC sessions in a fast three-column Mac interface built for scanning and repeated learning.

features.title: Synced playback
features.body: Watch video, read transcripts, inspect chapters, and open code snippets side by side.

features.title: Favorites
features.body: Save important sessions locally so you can return to them quickly.

features.title: Optional AI tools
features.body: Summarize, explain, draft questions, or start a blog outline using your own OpenAI-compatible credentials.

features.title: Local by default
features.body: Catalog data, transcript indexes, favorites, and settings are stored on your device.

privacy_sections.heading: Introduction
privacy_sections.body: Lectern - Watch & Learn respects your privacy. This policy describes what data the app stores, what it accesses, and how optional online features work.

privacy_sections.heading: Data collection
privacy_sections.body: Lectern does not collect personal data, usage analytics, tracking data, or crash reports for the developer.

privacy_sections.heading: Data stored on your device
list: The WWDC catalog cache, transcript search index, favorites, playback-related state, settings, and logs are stored locally on your device.
OpenAI-compatible API settings or sign-in tokens, if you choose to use AI features, are stored locally using the platform storage available to the app.

privacy_sections.heading: Apple developer content
privacy_sections.body: Lectern accesses publicly available Apple developer session metadata, videos, transcripts, chapters, and related materials so you can browse and search them inside the app. Lectern is not affiliated with Apple Inc.

privacy_sections.heading: Optional AI features
privacy_sections.body: AI features are optional. If you enable them, the prompts or transcript excerpts you choose to send may be transmitted to your selected OpenAI-compatible service provider to generate a response. Lectern does not receive or store those requests on a developer-operated server.

privacy_sections.heading: Data sharing
privacy_sections.body: Lectern does not sell your data and does not share your data with third parties for advertising or marketing.

notbadbookmark.name: NotBadBookmark
notbadbookmark.tagline: A modern replacement for Chrome's default bookmark manager.
notbadbookmark.description: If you save a lot of links, the built-in manager is painful. NotBadBookmark is a clean, fast extension with real search, drag-and-drop folders, and favicons that actually load. It uses Chrome's own bookmark store, so nothing leaks and nothing locks in.

features.title: Real search
features.body: Live filtering across titles and URLs, instant results.

features.title: Folder DnD
features.body: Drag bookmarks into folders, drag folders into folders.

features.title: Favicons that load
features.body: Smart favicon fetching so nothing shows the default puzzle piece.

features.title: Keyboard navigation
features.body: Arrow keys, enter, escape — get around without a mouse.

features.title: Light + dark
features.body: Follows your system theme.

features.title: No new account
features.body: Sits on top of Chrome's own bookmark store. Sync still works.

plainmd.name: PlainMD
plainmd.tagline: A focused Markdown editor for Android.
plainmd.description: A small, fast Markdown editor for Android. Write on the left, preview on the right (or one tap to switch). Files live wherever you tell them to via the system file picker — no folder lock-in, no cloud, no nag screens.

features.title: Edit / preview toggle
features.body: A single tap swaps between writing and reading mode.

features.title: Real Markdown
features.body: Tables, task lists, strikethrough, fenced code, LaTeX math.

features.title: Save anywhere
features.body: Uses the system file picker — keep notes in any folder or cloud you already use.

features.title: Adjustable type
features.body: Comfortable text sizes and themes for long writing sessions.

features.title: Syntax highlighting
features.body: Code blocks get language-aware coloring.

features.title: Autosave drafts
features.body: Never lose a paragraph to an accidental back-button.

remoboard.name: Remoboard
remoboard.tagline: Use your computer's keyboard to type on your iPhone.
remoboard.description: Long messages, snippets of code, anything you'd rather not thumb out — Remoboard pairs the iPhone keyboard with whatever real keyboard is on your desk, over your local network. Open the iOS app, connect from your computer, and start typing.

features.title: Cross-platform companion
features.body: Connect from macOS, Windows, or Linux. The iPhone is the receiver.

features.title: Local network only
features.body: Pairing stays on your Wi-Fi. Nothing routes through our servers.

features.title: Low latency
features.body: Type at full speed — keystrokes arrive on the iPhone with no perceptible lag.

features.title: Works in any app
features.body: Anywhere the iOS keyboard appears, Remoboard can type into it.

features.title: Quick pair
features.body: QR-code pairing, no manual IP juggling.

features.title: Free desktop apps
features.body: Companion apps for desktop are free downloads.

speaktwo.name: SpeakTwo
speaktwo.tagline: Real-time speech translation between two people.
speaktwo.description: SpeakTwo turns your iPhone into a shared interpreter. Set a language for each side, place the phone between you, and speak naturally — SpeakTwo transcribes and translates each turn live, powered by OpenAI's realtime translation model. Your API key stays on your device, so conversations go straight from your phone to OpenAI with no server in between.

features.title: Two-way conversation
features.body: Assign a language to each person and let SpeakTwo translate back and forth in a single shared session.

features.title: Realtime translation
features.body: Audio streams directly to OpenAI's gpt-realtime-translate model, so speech is transcribed and translated as you talk.

features.title: Bring your own key
features.body: Use your own OpenAI API key. It is stored locally in the iOS Keychain and never sent to a developer-operated server.

features.title: Native SwiftUI app
features.body: A focused, native iOS interface built for quick, in-person conversations.

features.title: Local history
features.body: Conversation transcripts can be saved on your device so you can review prior sessions.

features.title: Private by design
features.body: No developer-operated backend. Your audio and key go only to OpenAI using the credentials you provide.

privacy_sections.heading: Introduction
privacy_sections.body: SpeakTwo is a bring-your-own-key real-time translation app. This policy describes what data the app stores and how its translation feature works.

privacy_sections.heading: Data we collect
privacy_sections.body: SpeakTwo does not collect, sell, or share personal data through a developer-operated server.

privacy_sections.heading: OpenAI API key
privacy_sections.body: Your OpenAI API key is stored locally on your device in the iOS Keychain. SpeakTwo does not send your API key to any server controlled by the developer.

privacy_sections.heading: Speech and translation
privacy_sections.body: When you start a translation session, audio is sent directly from the app to OpenAI's realtime translation service using the API key you provide. OpenAI processes that audio to provide transcription and translation responses.

privacy_sections.heading: Local conversation history
privacy_sections.body: Conversation transcripts may be saved locally on your device so you can review prior sessions. These saved sessions are not uploaded to a developer-operated server.

privacy_sections.heading: Contact
privacy_sections.body: For privacy questions or support, open an issue at https://github.com/everettjf/SpeakTwo/issues or email xnuapp@gmail.com.

startmyapp.name: StartMyApp
startmyapp.tagline: The fastest way to find and launch apps on macOS.
startmyapp.description: StartMyApp lives in your menu bar and replaces the slow Spotlight dance. Press a shortcut, type a few characters, hit return. Apple Intelligence suggests what you usually open next, so most launches need just one keystroke.

links.label: Download on the Mac App Store

features.title: Instant launch
features.body: Open any installed application with a global shortcut and a few keystrokes.

features.title: Fuzzy search
features.body: Match by name, category, or aliases — typos and abbreviations are fine.

features.title: Apple Intelligence suggestions
features.body: Smart ranking learns the apps you actually use at this time of day.

features.title: Keyboard-first
features.body: Built for power users. The mouse is optional, end to end.

features.title: Categories & ordering
features.body: Group apps the way you think about them, not how the Finder sorts them.

features.title: Native and lightweight
features.body: A small Swift app. No Electron, no background bloat.

translayr.name: Translayr
translayr.tagline: System-wide translation across every macOS app, powered by local AI.
translayr.description: Select any text in any app, press a shortcut, get a clean translation overlay. Translayr runs on top of local Ollama models, so nothing leaves your machine — no API keys, no usage caps, no leaks of work-in-progress prose.

features.title: Works everywhere
features.body: Translates from any app — browser, IDE, Slack, PDFs, anywhere you can select text.

features.title: Local models
features.body: Plugs into Ollama. Your text never leaves the device.

features.title: 10 major languages
features.body: Bidirectional translation across English, Chinese, Japanese, and more.

features.title: Context-aware
features.body: The model sees enough surrounding text to keep tone and terminology consistent.

features.title: Fast overlay
features.body: A focused popover, no app switching, no clipboard juggling.

features.title: Customizable shortcut
features.body: Bind it to whatever key you already think of as 'translate'.

wiresnap.name: WireSnap
wiresnap.tagline: Learn any language from photos and screenshots.
wiresnap.description: Point your camera at a sign, menu, or webpage. WireSnap recognizes the words, shows translations and parts of speech, and saves anything you want to remember. The fastest way to turn the world around you into a vocabulary deck.

features.title: AI vision
features.body: Reads handwriting, signs, screenshots, and printed text in dozens of languages.

features.title: One-tap vocab
features.body: Tap any word in a photo to see meaning, pronunciation, and example uses.

features.title: Bring your own key
features.body: Use your own OpenAI / Anthropic key for unlimited usage, or stay on the free plan.

features.title: Personal deck
features.body: Save words to a deck for spaced review later.

features.title: Works on screenshots
features.body: Captures from games, web pages, and other apps work the same as photos.

features.title: Private
features.body: Images stay on device by default; only the OCR text is sent to the model you chose.

privacy_sections.heading: Introduction
privacy_sections.body: WireSnap respects your privacy. This document describes what data the app collects and how it is used.

privacy_sections.heading: Information we collect
list: Images and screenshots you choose to process.
API keys you supply for Bring-Your-Own-Key usage.
Anonymous usage data and crash reports.

privacy_sections.heading: How we use information
list: To run OCR and translation on the images you submit.
To respond to questions you send to us directly.

privacy_sections.heading: Third-party services
privacy_sections.body: When BYOK is enabled, your images and OCR output go straight to the API provider you configured. We do not relay or store that data on our servers.

privacy_sections.heading: Data security
privacy_sections.body: Where data is transmitted off-device, it is sent over TLS. API keys are stored encrypted in the system keychain.

privacy_sections.heading: Data retention
privacy_sections.body: We keep only what is needed to provide the service. You can clear your saved deck and remove API keys at any time inside the app.

wizvoid.name: Wizvoid
wizvoid.tagline: Run large language models entirely offline, on your phone.
wizvoid.description: Wizvoid runs open-source language models directly on iOS through Swift MLX. Pick a model, download it once, and chat without an internet connection. Everything you type and everything the model says stays on the device.

features.title: 18+ local models
features.body: From small instruct models to larger ones — pick what fits your device.

features.title: Powered by MLX
features.body: Apple's MLX framework runs efficiently on the Neural Engine.

features.title: Private by default
features.body: No network calls. No telemetry. No accounts.

features.title: Conversational
features.body: Chat threads with proper context windows and history.

features.title: Model switching
features.body: Swap between models mid-session to compare answers.

features.title: Works on a plane
features.body: Once a model is downloaded, no connection is needed.
